[TITLE]: Man Wrestles 7-Foot Shark to Nantucket Beach | National Geographic
[METADATA/INTRO]: Man Wrestles 7-Foot Shark to Nantucket Beach Surf fisherman promotes catch-and-release but still angers shark advocates....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Man Wrestles 7-Foot Shark to Nantucket Beach 
 Surf fisherman promotes catch-and-release but still angers shark advocates. 
 When Elliot Sudal goes to the beach, he doesn't just lay out in the sun. He wrestles sharks. 
 On July 14, Sudal spent 45 minutes writhing in the Nantucket surf with a seven-foot (two-meter) sandbar shark. Eventually, he got the animal to shore, then released it a few moments later. The encounter was recorded by his companions and posted online, where it has gone viral. A number of shark advocates have expressed concern about potential injury to the animal. 
 "I pull them on shore, I photograph them, and then I let them go, I'm pretty conservation minded, I'm not trying to eat them or hurt them," Sudal told National Geographic.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Great white among sharks caught from Florida panhandle beaches | wwltv.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: In the world of social media, news of landing massive-sized mako and tiger sharks on our Northwest Florida beaches is becoming more common. No one, however, ever expected to see photos and video of anglers reeling in a great white...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In the world of social media, news of landing massive-sized mako and tiger sharks on our Northwest Florida beaches is becoming more common. 
 No one, however, ever expected to see photos and video of anglers reeling in a great white onto a popular spring break beach. 
 Cue the "da-dum, da-dum." 
 Shark anglers Derrick Keeny and Kyle Register, with the help of Gabriel Smeby, didn't even need a "bigger boat" — just a rod and reel and muscle power to wrangle the nearly 9 foot, 8 1/2-incher onto a Panama City Beach Sunday night. 
 The catch is earning them the bragging rights of landing the first documented great white off a Florida Panhandle beach, though Santa Rosa County land-based shark angler Earnie Polk said two local men, Elmo Horney and Rody Pate, caught one off of Navarre Beach pier in the 1960s. They donated its jaw to what's now Pensacola State College, he said.

[TITLE]: Catch of a lifetime: Fishermen reel in Great white shark in Outer Banks
[METADATA/INTRO]: Catch of a lifetime: Fishermen reel in Great white shark in Outer Banks In an extraordinary tale sure to hook any fishing enthusiast, seasoned anglers Luke Beard and Jason Rosenfeld netted a lifetime achievement on the shores of Hatteras Island,...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Catch of a lifetime: Fishermen reel in Great white shark in Outer Banks 
 In an extraordinary tale sure to hook any fishing enthusiast, seasoned anglers Luke Beard and Jason Rosenfeld netted a lifetime achievement on the shores of Hatteras Island, North Carolina on March 15 when the men encountered a massive great white shark. 
 "I've been dreaming about this," Beard told WRAL News. "For like two years, we had told everybody we were going to do it so we did it finally." 
 Beard and Rosenfeld faced the heart-stopping moment that was captured in a now-viral video. The footage shows the two men, along with five others, wrestling the enormous predator in shallow waters after reeling it in and then immediately working to safely release it back into the ocean.
